entities.name: NITRIFICATION
entities.description: The biological process in soil that converts ammonium-nitrogen to nitrate-nitrogen via nitrite

entities.name: SOIL
entities.description: The medium where nitrification occurs and where nitrogen is stored

entities.name: AMMONIUM-NITROGEN
entities.description: A relatively immobile form of nitrogen that serves as the starting material for nitrification

entities.name: NITRATE-NITROGEN
entities.description: A highly mobile form of nitrogen produced during the nitrification process

entities.name: NITRITE
entities.description: An intermediate compound in the conversion of ammonium-nitrogen to nitrate-nitrogen

entities.name: N-USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which agricultural systems utilize nitrogen, affected by nitrification

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY
entities.description: The state of the environment, which can be negatively impacted by the loss of nitrogen following nitrate formation

entities.name: SOIL MATRIX
entities.description: A physical factor that exerts a strong influence on the process of nitrification

entities.name: WATER STATUS
entities.description: An environmental factor that exerts a strong influence on the process of nitrification

entities.name: AERATION
entities.description: A physical factor relating to oxygen availability that exerts a strong influence on nitrification

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: A physical factor that exerts a strong influence on the process of nitrification

entities.name: PH
entities.description: A chemical factor that exerts a strong influence on the process of nitrification

entities.name: NITRIFICATION INHIBITORS
entities.description: Chemical or biological agents employed to regulate the process of nitrification in soils
